rationale: A0 shows PowerShell launched with '-WindowStyle hidden' executing a DownloadFile call to a bit.ly-shortened URL ('http://bit.ly/L3g1tCrad1e') — a classic cradle pattern used to obfuscate the true download destination and stage a payload. EV-003 confirms the download succeeded: the same PowerShell process (PID 1668) created 'Default_File_Path.ps1' in the Administrator's home directory, meaning a remote script is now on disk. EV-002 shows a PSScriptPolicyTest temp file, consistent with PowerShell evaluating execution policy before running the dropped script. EV-001 establishes the parent chain: an interactive cmd.exe spawned from Explorer under the high-integrity Administrator account, indicating either direct console access or a prior interactive compromise; no benign business justification is evident for a hidden PowerShell download cradle using a URL shortener.
